sidebar.uploadDemoBody: Demo: upload weekly notes, report cards, or PDFs so the AI can reference them in chat.
sidebar.practiceDemoBody: Demo: create 2–3 short items from the current learning-card topic for the student to try.
sidebar.snippetsDemoBody: Demo: one-tap inserts like “Explain shorter” or “Give a real-life example” into the composer.

parent.dashboard: Learning cards, your child’s week, and mood snapshot.
parent.chat: School notices, chat with the teacher, and book a one-to-one slot.
parent.knowledge: Pick a learning card and chat with BridgeEd AI about that topic—definitions, practice ideas, and next steps.
parent.mood: See your child’s weekly mood summary and trends.

student.chat: Class notices and messages from your teacher.
student.knowledge: Pick a class learning card and chat with BridgeEd AI about that topic—practice ideas and quick explanations.
student.mood: Pick how you feel, tag what influenced your mood, and submit—your parent can see the weekly summary on the dashboard.

teacher.dashboard: Create class reports and push them to students and parents under Messages—plus tasks, posts, and your week.
teacher.chat: Broadcast to class or parents and manage booking requests.
teacher.mood: Class mood overview alongside learning feedback.

hints.dashboardFallback: Class report, learning cards, tasks, posts, class pulse, directory, and this week’s schedule.

knowledge.emptyStudent: No learning cards yet. When your teacher sends a card to your class, it will show up here.
knowledge.emptyParent: No learning cards yet. Open the dashboard to see cards your teacher shared.
knowledge.composerPlaceholder: Ask about this card’s topic, or paste a homework question…
knowledge.demoThread: Open this card from the dashboard to start the AI thread.
knowledge.studentNoChildContent: This card doesn’t have kid-friendly media picks yet. Ask BridgeEd AI below—or your teacher can add student-facing content later.
knowledge.lcStepsEmpty: No suggested steps on this card.
knowledge.parentDoNotUnderstandPrompt: I still don't understand this topic. Please explain it more simply or suggest another way to practice it with my child.
knowledge.parentDoNotUnderstandAria: Insert a draft message asking for help with this topic
knowledge.challengeFeedbackSubtitle: What felt boring, hard, or not your thing? One short sentence is enough — your teacher can help.
knowledge.challengeFeedbackPlaceholder: e.g. Too long · Want a different topic · Not sure where to start…
knowledge.challengeFeedbackRequired: Please add a note so we understand.

practice.mockReply:
**Mini practice**

Let’s use **Van Gogh’s approach** for a quick exercise: **paint a sunflower**.

- **Brushwork:** Use thick paint and short strokes—leave visible marks like Van Gogh; it doesn’t need to be perfectly smooth.
- **Color:** Bright yellow as the main hue; add a little deep green and ochre for the center and shadows.
- **Head and petals:** Start with a flat oval for the flower head, then use radiating strokes for petal direction—add a hint of swirl or movement if you like.

When you’re done, share the three colors you used, what you’d tweak next—or upload a photo.

cardPreview.demoHint: Sample engagement data for layout preview — will connect to live class data later.
cardPreview.moduleProgressLede: How students are distributed across TODO, DOING, and DONE on this card.
cardPreview.moduleFeedbackLede: Signals from parents and from students who finished the card.
cardPreview.studentFeedbackPanelLede: {{count}} students marked DONE; breakdown of how they felt:
cardPreview.studentFeedbackEmpty: No self-report yet from students who finished.

reportModal.title: Create & push report
reportModal.lede: Recipients get a copy in Messages. Import reference files (exam papers, scans) for your records — file names are listed for your checklist. Attach PDFs, Word docs, or images.
reportModal.aiUploadCallout: Uploaded content is analyzed by AI to extract key points and help generate this report.
reportModal.fieldSummaryPh: Short headline for families: overall tone, one key win, one focus area…
reportModal.fieldBodyPh: Highlights, reminders, optional next steps, and anything to push to chat…
reportModal.detailsHint: Sample analytics for layout — connect to your gradebook and learning-card data later.
reportModal.learningCardsStat: {{opened}} of {{total}} students opened at least one learning card.
reportModal.previewLede: This is how your summary and message will read for the audience you selected. Go back to edit, or send to Messages.
reportModal.previewSuccess: Sent. Students and/or parents will see this under Messages.

wizard.title: New learning card
wizard.lede: Draft a parent-friendly card from your class notes, then choose who receives it.
wizard.subject: Subject
wizard.studentPreviewBody: From grade, subject, topic, and your notes below, we build a student-facing page: ordered steps with short explanations, plus suggested videos where they help most.
wizard.studentPreviewParentHint: You’ll still edit a parent-friendly summary in the next step before sending.
wizard.notesPlaceholder: e.g. steps to stress, common mistakes, or topics to de-emphasize…
wizard.generatingHint: Parent summary and student video discovery (for Knowledge) from your class context.
wizard.aiHint: Families will see this in their preferred language — we translate the summary automatically for each parent.
wizard.childErrorFollowUp: Go back and try generating again.
wizard.studentEmpty: Nothing generated for students yet. Go back and run generate again.
wizard.confirmLead: You’re about to send this learning card.
wizard.confirmCount: {{count}} {{familyWord}} will get a notification under Knowledge powered by BridgeEd AI.
